pi_items.source: Task description (line 5): '**New**: Set Probability to **5%**' matches metadata value of 5.0. Verified via setup_task.sh which creates these stages and verifier.py which checks probability tolerance.

Task description (line 5): 'Requirements: 'Initial contact established. Lead source identified.' contains both required phrases. Verifier checks for case-insensitive substring presence (verifier.py:108).
Task description (line 5): '**Qualified**: Set Probability to **30%**' matches metadata value of 30.0. Verified via setup_task.sh reset and verifier.py float tolerance check.

Task description (line 5): 'Requirements: 'Budget confirmed. Decision maker identified. Need established. Timeline defined.' contains all three required phrases. Verifier checks for substring presence.
Task description (line 5): '**Proposition**: Set Probability to **65%**' matches metadata value of 65.0.

Task description (line 5): 'Requirements: 'Proposal delivered. Pricing reviewed by customer. Legal or procurement involved.' contains both required phrases. Verifier substring matching confirms these are sufficient.
Task description (line 5): '**Won**: Set Probability to **100%**' matches metadata value of 100.0. This is standard for Odoo CRM won stage.
Task description (line 5): 'Requirements: 'Contract signed. Purchase order received.' contains both required phrases.

privileged_info_summary: Task edit_stage_probabilities@1 uses synthetic data within a containerized Odoo 17 CRM environment. The task requires users to update four CRM pipeline stages with specific probability values: New (5%), Qualified (30%), Proposition (65%), and Won (100%). Each stage has associated requirements text with minimum required phrases verified via case-insensitive substring matching. All metadata values have been verified against the task description and are consistent with the setup scripts (setup_task.sh creates/resets stages via XMLRPC) and verifier logic (verifier.py validates with 0.1 float tolerance for probabilities and substring matching for requirements text).
